items.briefing:
A cyberattack on Mackay Sugar has raised concerns about vulnerabilities in Australia's food supply chain, highlighting that critical industries remain attractive targets. Separately, research shows one in three IT assets in organisations lack basic security controls — a reminder that gaps in your own workplace systems may be going unnoticed. Australians are also being warned about cyber scams targeting FIFA World Cup fans, including fake ticket websites and phishing links.

From 1 July, some text messages will look different due to new sender ID rules designed to reduce scam SMS. If you receive an unexpected message asking you to click a link or provide details, verify it through the organisation's official website before acting.

featured.title: Mackay Sugar cyber attack flagged as broader risk to Australia’s food supply chain
featured.quote: Critical infrastructure attack on major Australian sugar exporter highlights food supply chain vulnerability.
